Title: Metabolic co-dependence drives the evolutionarily ancient Hydra–Chlorella symbiosis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Symbiosis-specific upregulation suggests enhanced phosphate uptake/supply in Hydra hosting native Chlorella A99, likely contributing to phosphorus provision within the symbiosis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hydra endodermal epithelial cells hosting symbionts
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed specifically in endoderm; strongly up-regulated in symbiotic animals; induced by light/photosynthetic activity and by high maltose/glucose treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hydra microarray; qPCR; whole-mount in situ hybridization; endoderm/ectoderm tissue qPCR; dark treatment; DCMU photosynthesis inhibition; sugar feeding assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Presence of native Chlorella A99; light versus dark; DCMU; maltose or glucose supplementation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Na/Pi transporter transcript rc_26218 is specifically up-regulated in Hydra with native Chlorella A99 versus aposymbiotic and NC64A-associated animals. 2. Expression is localized to the endoderm, where symbionts reside. 3. Upregulation depends on algal photosynthetic activity and can be induced by sugars, supporting a role in nutrient exchange, likely phosphate supply, in symbiosis.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Hydra custom microarray GPL23280; expression dataset GSE97633
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of the most specific host response genes to native symbiosis; interpreted by authors as potentially controlling intracellular phosphate levels and contributing phosphorus to Chlorella.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Spot14 is specifically induced by native symbiont photosynthesis and by exogenous maltose/glucose, marking host metabolic activation associated with photosynthate-dependent symbiosis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in both ectoderm and endoderm; strongly up-regulated in symbiotic animals; light- and sugar-inducible
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray; qPCR; whole-mount in situ hybridization; tissue-specific qPCR; dark treatment; DCMU treatment; sugar feeding assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Presence of Chlorella A99; light versus dark; DCMU; maltose and glucose supplementation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Spot14 is an A99-specific host gene induced in the presence of native symbionts. 2. Its expression requires photosynthetic activity and is mimicked by maltose or glucose addition. 3. The gene is expressed in both tissue layers, indicating systemic host response to algal photosynthate.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although nutritionally relevant, Spot14 is used mainly as a marker of photosynthate-responsive host metabolism rather than being functionally validated as a direct metabolic effector in this paper.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulated in symbiotic Hydra, suggesting enhanced host uptake/transfer of photosynthate-derived glucose from symbionts.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in symbiotic Hydra versus aposymbiotic Hydra
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Solute carrier family 2 facilitated glucose transporter
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A GLUT8-like transporter is up-regulated in Hydra hosting symbiotic Chlorella. 2. The authors infer it may mediate transport of photosynthate from symbiont to host. 3. This mirrors observations from other cnidarian-algal symbioses.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inference is based on expression and homology; no direct transport assay was performed.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: glutamine and other neutral amino acids
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expanded copy number in symbiotic Chlorella A99 supports increased amino-acid import capacity and dependence on host-derived nitrogenous amino acids.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome sequencing; Pfam domain search; ortholog group comparison across algae
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chlorella A99 contains many amino acid permease 2 orthologs. 2. This expansion is greater than in several free-living algae and consistent with glutamine utilization. 3. The finding supports metabolic dependence on host-supplied amino acids.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Chlorella sp. A99 genome BioProject PRJNA412448; assembly accession PCFQ00000000
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats these as candidate factors inferred from comparative genomics, not individually functionally validated genes.

Common_Genes.Gene_Name: SLC38-like transmembrane amino acid transporter family protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: glutamine and other neutral amino acids
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enrichment of SLC38-like amino acid transporters in Chlorella A99 indicates enhanced uptake of host-derived neutral amino acids during symbiosis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome sequencing; Pfam search; ortholog comparison
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aa_trans domain; sodium-coupled neutral amino acid transporter family
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chlorella A99 encodes multiple SLC38-like neutral amino acid transporters. 2. Copy number is elevated in symbiotic Chlorella relative to several free-living algae. 3. This supports dependence on imported amino acids, including glutamine.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Chlorella sp. A99 genome BioProject PRJNA412448; assembly accession PCFQ00000000
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Comparative-genomic inference only; no single gene was experimentally dissected.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Host ammonium assimilation to glutamine in symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses glutamate and ammonium to produce glutamine in an ATP-dependent reaction.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the nitrogenous metabolite proposed to be supplied from host to symbiont
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step in host-mediated glutamine provision to the symbiont
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen source supporting growth of symbiotic Chlorella A99
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hydra endoderm and ectoderm; functionally linked to symbiosome-associated host cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GS-1 is specifically induced by native Chlorella A99, by light/photosynthesis, and by maltose/glucose, supporting host glutamine synthesis for symbiont nutrition.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in both ectoderm and endoderm; strongly up-regulated in symbiotic animals; specifically GS-1 among three Hydra GS genes is induced
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray; qPCR; phylogenetic analysis of GS genes; whole-mount in situ hybridization; tissue-specific qPCR; dark treatment; DCMU treatment; sugar feeding assays; algal growth assay with glutamine supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Presence of native Chlorella A99; light versus dark; DCMU; maltose/glucose supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GS-1 is one of the most specific Hydra genes induced by native symbiotic Chlorella A99. 2. Its induction depends on algal photosynthesis and can be mimicked by maltose or glucose. 3. Only GS-1, but not other Hydra GS paralogs, responds to symbiosis. 4. Glutamine supplementation temporarily supports in vitro growth of Chlorella A99, supporting the model that host GS-1-derived glutamine nourishes the symbiont.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper’s central nutrient-metabolism model links photosynthate release by Chlorella to host GS-1 activation and glutamine provision back to the alga.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Represents a metabolic pathway down-regulated in host by native symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Down-regulated specifically in Hydra with native Chlorella A99, indicating altered host carbon metabolism during symbiosis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in Hv_Sym compared with Hv_Apo and Hv_NC64A
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Acetoacetyl-CoA synthetase is specifically down-regulated in native symbiosis. 2. This supports broad remodeling of host metabolism by the symbiont. 3. Direct causal contribution to a nutrient exchange phenotype was not functionally tested.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among A99-specific metabolic genes, but peripheral to the main glutamine/phosphate exchange model.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Represents host amino acid metabolic pathway down-regulated in native symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically down-regulated in Hydra hosting native Chlorella A99, consistent with reduced host amino acid metabolic activity in symbiosis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in Hv_Sym versus Hv_Apo and Hv_NC64A
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 2-isopropylmalate synthase is among the A99-specific down-regulated host metabolic genes. 2. The result supports host metabolic reprogramming in response to native symbiosis. 3. No direct causal metabolite test was presented.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Annotation in an animal may reflect homology-based assignment; functional identity in Hydra was not biochemically confirmed.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Represents host amino acid catabolism down-regulated by native symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically down-regulated in native symbiosis, consistent with altered host amino acid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in Hv_Sym relative to Hv_Apo and Hv_NC64A
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Histidine ammonia-lyase is specifically down-regulated in Hydra hosting native symbionts. 2. The result contributes to the evidence that amino acid metabolism is remodeled during symbiosis. 3. The study did not directly test metabolite flux through this step.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Peripheral to the central glutamine supply model.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates high-affinity transport of nitrate/nitrite into the cell.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for inorganic nitrogen assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key uptake step; absent in A99
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Absence of NRT2 in Chlorella A99 supports inability to utilize nitrate efficiently and dependence on host-derived amino acids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome sequencing; ortholog search; reciprocal BLAST; PCR validation of absence; in vitro growth assays on nitrate
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Chlorella sp. A99 genome BioProject PRJNA412448; assembly accession PCFQ00000000
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss likely constrains free-living growth on inorganic nitrogen
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chlorella A99 lacks detectable NRT2 genes by comparative genomics and PCR. 2. This loss is consistent with failure of A99 to grow on nitrate in vitro. 3. The result supports evolutionary degeneration of the nitrate assimilation pathway in obligate symbiosis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although absent rather than manipulated, NRT2 is central to the paper’s nutrient-dependence model.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Accessory component required for functionality of some high-affinity nitrate transport systems.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for precursor uptake system leading to nitrogen assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key accessory component; absent in A99
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Absence of NAR2 further indicates collapse of the high-affinity nitrate assimilation apparatus in Chlorella A99.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Absent from genome by comparative search
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome sequencing; ortholog search; reciprocal BLAST
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Chlorella sp. A99 genome BioProject PRJNA412448; assembly accession PCFQ00000000
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAR2 could not be identified in the Chlorella A99 genome. 2. Its loss complements the absence of NRT2 and supports degeneration of high-affinity nitrate uptake. 3. This genomic pattern is consistent with amino-acid dependence in symbiosis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PCR was not done due to weak conservation, so evidence is genome-search based.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces nitrite to ammonium using reduced ferredoxin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Produces ammonium precursor for glutamine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential pathway enzyme; absent in A99
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Absence of NiR in Chlorella A99 indicates inability to complete nitrate-to-ammonium assimilation, reinforcing dependence on host-provided glutamine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome sequencing; ortholog search; reciprocal BLAST; PCR validation of absence; in vitro growth assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Chlorella sp. A99 genome BioProject PRJNA412448; assembly accession PCFQ00000000
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss blocks nitrate assimilation and likely constrains autonomous growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NiR is absent from the Chlorella A99 genome and not amplifiable by PCR. 2. This breaks the nitrate assimilation pathway downstream of nitrate reductase. 3. Together with culture data, the absence supports obligate reliance on organic nitrogen sources such as glutamine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the strongest genomic findings linking algal metabolism to host dependence.
